item.description: The fire began in a vape shop on Union Street on Sunday afternoon before the building collapsed hours later.

item.title: The rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader has been shaped by decades-long ties with the powerful and hardline Revolutionary Guards

item.title:
HISTORIC SWINGS ROCK OIL MARKET
Today’s move in Brent crude prices is among the most dramatic in history.
🔸Brent recorded its largest intraday gain ever, jumping $26.81 — even bigger than the surge during the 2022 oil spike when prices briefly hit $139.
🔸If prices close below $99, it would mark the biggest drop from an intraday high to the close on record.
🔸The market is also heading toward the second-largest daily high-low range ever; it would take a drop to $93.46 to set a new record.
Bottom line: today ranks as one of the most volatile days ever for global oil prices.
item.description:
HISTORIC SWINGS ROCK OIL MARKET

Today’s move in Brent crude prices is among the most dramatic in history.

🔸Brent recorded its largest intraday gain ever, jumping $26.81 — even bigger than the surge during the 2022 oil spike when prices briefly hit $139.

🔸If prices close below $99, it would mark the biggest drop from an intraday high to the close on record.

🔸The market is also heading toward the second-largest daily high-low range ever; it would take a drop to $93.46 to set a new record.

Bottom line: today ranks as one of the most volatile days ever for global oil prices.

item.title:
Pinned: MIDTERM ODDS CROSS THE LINE
As of March 2026 on Kalshi, traders give Democrats a 45% chance of controlling both the House and Senate after the 2026 midterms. An all time high. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxbalancepowercombo/congress-balance-of-power-combo/kxbalancepowercombo-27feb?utm_source=walterbloomberg

As of March 2026 on Kalshi, traders give Democrats a 45% chance of controlling both the House and Senate after the 2026 midterms. An all time high.
kalshi.com/markets/kxbalance…

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS IT’S “TOO SOON” TO DISCUSS SEIZING IRAN’S OIL
President Donald Trump declined to commit to seizing Iranian oil as the U.S. military campaign against Iran continues, saying it’s “too soon to talk about that,” though he acknowledged the idea has been discussed publicly and compared it to U.S. actions in Venezuela.
Trump, speaking to NBC News, avoided saying whether he wants the U.S. to take control of Iranian crude but noted that people have considered it — similar to how Washington moved to secure Venezuelan oil after capturing President Nicolás Maduro.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS IT’S “TOO SOON” TO DISCUSS SEIZING IRAN’S OIL

President Donald Trump declined to commit to seizing Iranian oil as the U.S. military campaign against Iran continues, saying it’s “too soon to talk about that,” though he acknowledged the idea has been discussed publicly and compared it to U.S. actions in Venezuela.

Trump, speaking to NBC News, avoided saying whether he wants the U.S. to take control of Iranian crude but noted that people have considered it — similar to how Washington moved to secure Venezuelan oil after capturing President Nicolás Maduro.

item.title:
U.S. FARMERS SEEK HELP AS FERTILIZER PRICES SOAR
The Strait of Hormuz disruption is sending fertilizer costs sharply higher, straining U.S. farmers ahead of planting season.
Zippy Duvall urged President Donald Trump to:
🔸Use the U.S. Navy to secure maritime fertilizer shipments.
🔸Lift import duties on certain fertilizers, including from Morocco and Russia.
Prices are climbing fast: urea +12% to $611/ton, potash +9%, ammonia +15%, while grain futures, including soybeans, hit multi-year highs.
item.description:
U.S. FARMERS SEEK HELP AS FERTILIZER PRICES SOAR

The Strait of Hormuz disruption is sending fertilizer costs sharply higher, straining U.S. farmers ahead of planting season.

Zippy Duvall urged President Donald Trump to:

🔸Use the U.S. Navy to secure maritime fertilizer shipments.

🔸Lift import duties on certain fertilizers, including from Morocco and Russia.

Prices are climbing fast: urea +12% to $611/ton, potash +9%, ammonia +15%, while grain futures, including soybeans, hit multi-year highs.

item.title:
ISRAELI OFFICIALS ARE GROWING CONCERNED: WAPO
A few senior officials in Israel are starting to voice concern about the escalating, open-ended attack on Iran — and suggesting possible exit ramps that might halt the war before it further damages the region and the global economy.
item.description:
ISRAELI OFFICIALS ARE GROWING CONCERNED: WAPO

A few senior officials in Israel are starting to voice concern about the escalating, open-ended attack on Iran — and suggesting possible exit ramps that might halt the war before it further damages the region and the global economy.

item.title:
🚨 IRAN WARNS IT’S READY FOR LONG CONFLICT
A senior Iranian official, Kamal Kharazi, told CNN that Iran is prepared for a prolonged war with the U.S. and will continue strikes on Gulf countries to pressure allies to push President Trump to step back.
Kharazi said diplomacy is off the table, with the war expected to end only through economic pain that forces other nations to intervene.
Iran’s attacks have disrupted Gulf energy trade, collapsed maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, and pushed crude prices above $100 a barrel, rattling global markets.
item.description:
🚨 IRAN WARNS IT’S READY FOR LONG CONFLICT

A senior Iranian official, Kamal Kharazi, told CNN that Iran is prepared for a prolonged war with the U.S. and will continue strikes on Gulf countries to pressure allies to push President Trump to step back.

Kharazi said diplomacy is off the table, with the war expected to end only through economic pain that forces other nations to intervene.

Iran’s attacks have disrupted Gulf energy trade, collapsed maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, and pushed crude prices above $100 a barrel, rattling global markets.

item.title: *MACRON: IRAN WAR TO LAST PERHAPS SEVERAL WEEKS
item.description: *MACRON: IRAN WAR TO LAST PERHAPS SEVERAL WEEKS

item.title: Oil’s extreme intraday moves are challenging conventional bull and bear market labels. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude, the U.S. benchmark, recently dipped to around $95 a barrel, over 20% below its intraday peak above $119.
item.description: Oil’s extreme intraday moves are challenging conventional bull and bear market labels. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude, the U.S. benchmark, recently dipped to around $95 a barrel, over 20% below its intraday peak above $119.

item.title:
HORMUZ HALT FORCES OPEC+ TO CUT OUTPUT
The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz has forced Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Iraq, and Kuwait to cut crude production, taking roughly 6.2–6.9mn b/d offline due to storage limits and blocked exports.
🔸Saudi Arabia shut offshore fields (Safaniya, Marjan, Zuluf, Abu Safa), cutting 2–2.5mn b/d; using Yanbu and the East-West pipeline as alternatives.
🔸Iraq cut output from 4.42mn b/d in February to 1.2–1.3mn b/d, redirecting crude to domestic refineries; northern pipeline to Turkey also closed.
🔸Kuwait reduced production from 2.59mn b/d to around 2mn, may drop to 1.5mn b/d; refineries operating at ~50% capacity.
🔸UAE lowered production to 2.7–3mn b/d, partially diverting crude via Adcop pipeline (1.7–1.8mn b/d) and domestic refining.
🔸Bahrain declared force majeure after refinery attack.
The disruptions have pushed Brent crude above $100/bbl, reflecting the loss of major Middle East Gulf supply.
item.description:
HORMUZ HALT FORCES OPEC+ TO CUT OUTPUT

The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz has forced Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Iraq, and Kuwait to cut crude production, taking roughly 6.2–6.9mn b/d offline due to storage limits and blocked exports.

🔸Saudi Arabia shut offshore fields (Safaniya, Marjan, Zuluf, Abu Safa), cutting 2–2.5mn b/d; using Yanbu and the East-West pipeline as alternatives.

🔸Iraq cut output from 4.42mn b/d in February to 1.2–1.3mn b/d, redirecting crude to domestic refineries; northern pipeline to Turkey also closed.

🔸Kuwait reduced production from 2.59mn b/d to around 2mn, may drop to 1.5mn b/d; refineries operating at ~50% capacity.

🔸UAE lowered production to 2.7–3mn b/d, partially diverting crude via Adcop pipeline (1.7–1.8mn b/d) and domestic refining.

🔸Bahrain declared force majeure after refinery attack.

The disruptions have pushed Brent crude above $100/bbl, reflecting the loss of major Middle East Gulf supply.

item.title:
GOLDMAN: OIL MAY HIT DEMAND-DESTRUCTION LEVELS SOONER
Goldman Sachs warns that with Strait of Hormuz shipments down 90%, removing ~18% of global oil supply, prices may need to rise to demand-destruction levels faster than historical models suggest.
Key points: 🔸Brent briefly topped $100/bbl, settling near $99; WTI near $96. 🔸Middle East pipeline redirection is only 25% effective; shippers mostly in “wait-and-see” mode. 🔸Qatari LNG disruption could last longer, pushing 2Q26 TTF gas forecasts to $22/mmBtu. 🔸Energy equities see sharp dispersion, with U.S., European, and Asian oil/gas stocks reacting to higher crude and refined product prices.
item.description:
GOLDMAN: OIL MAY HIT DEMAND-DESTRUCTION LEVELS SOONER

Goldman Sachs warns that with Strait of Hormuz shipments down 90%, removing ~18% of global oil supply, prices may need to rise to demand-destruction levels faster than historical models suggest.

Key points:
🔸Brent briefly topped $100/bbl, settling near $99; WTI near $96.
🔸Middle East pipeline redirection is only 25% effective; shippers mostly in “wait-and-see” mode.
🔸Qatari LNG disruption could last longer, pushing 2Q26 TTF gas forecasts to $22/mmBtu.
🔸Energy equities see sharp dispersion, with U.S., European, and Asian oil/gas stocks reacting to higher crude and refined product prices.

item.description: Concern has grown for team after one critic called them 'wartime traitors' for failing to salute during the Iranian anthem.

item.title: TRUMP MULLS EXPORT CURBS, TAX WAIVERS TO CURB OIL PRICES
item.description: TRUMP MULLS EXPORT CURBS, TAX WAIVERS TO CURB OIL PRICES

item.title:
🚨EXCLUSIVE- TRUMP WEIGHS OPTIONS AS OIL HITS $100
President Donald Trump is reviewing measures to curb oil above $100 a barrel amid the Iran war, ahead of the November midterms.
Options under discussion with the G7 and U.S. agencies include releasing strategic reserves, limiting exports, intervening in futures, waiving taxes, and easing the Jones Act.
Analysts warn these steps may have little impact while Middle East oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz—which carries 20% of global oil—remain blocked.
Global crude briefly touched $119 a barrel, pushing gasoline costs higher. The White House is coordinating with top officials, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and adviser Stephen Miller, but naval escorts and insurance for tankers have so far failed to ease the disruption.
item.description:
🚨EXCLUSIVE- TRUMP WEIGHS OPTIONS AS OIL HITS $100

President Donald Trump is reviewing measures to curb oil above $100 a barrel amid the Iran war, ahead of the November midterms.

Options under discussion with the G7 and U.S. agencies include releasing strategic reserves, limiting exports, intervening in futures, waiving taxes, and easing the Jones Act.

Analysts warn these steps may have little impact while Middle East oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz—which carries 20% of global oil—remain blocked.

Global crude briefly touched $119 a barrel, pushing gasoline costs higher. The White House is coordinating with top officials, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and adviser Stephen Miller, but naval escorts and insurance for tankers have so far failed to ease the disruption.

item.title:
ANALYSTS SEE BRENT OIL SURGING TO $150
Banks and research firms are raising oil forecasts amid ongoing Iran conflict and a potential prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz:
🔸Barclays: Brent could test $120 if fighting continues for weeks.
🔸Capital Economics: Brent may reach $150 mid-2026 in a worst-case scenario before easing to $130.
🔸Rystad Energy: Brent could spike to $135 if conflict lasts four months.
🔸Macquarie: Hormuz closure could push crude to $150+.
🔸Goldman Sachs: Will revisit forecasts if Hormuz flows don’t normalize; Q2 2026 average previously $76.
item.description:
ANALYSTS SEE BRENT OIL SURGING TO $150

Banks and research firms are raising oil forecasts amid ongoing Iran conflict and a potential prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz:

🔸Barclays: Brent could test $120 if fighting continues for weeks.

🔸Capital Economics: Brent may reach $150 mid-2026 in a worst-case scenario before easing to $130.

🔸Rystad Energy: Brent could spike to $135 if conflict lasts four months.

🔸Macquarie: Hormuz closure could push crude to $150+.

🔸Goldman Sachs: Will revisit forecasts if Hormuz flows don’t normalize; Q2 2026 average previously $76.

item.title:
OIL COULD JUMP 50% IF MIDEAST WAR SPREADS
Edward Morse warns that crude prices could rise 50% if Middle East fighting widens. He notes the U.S. lags in feeling the impact, but gasoline could hit $4 a gallon as oil surges.
item.description:
OIL COULD JUMP 50% IF MIDEAST WAR SPREADS

Edward Morse warns that crude prices could rise 50% if Middle East fighting widens. He notes the U.S. lags in feeling the impact, but gasoline could hit $4 a gallon as oil surges.

item.title: US missile hit military base near Iran school, video analysis shows
item.description: A US Tomahawk missile hit a military base near a primary school in southern Iran where Iranian authorities said 168 people were killed, expert video analysis shows.

item.title: G7 ‘stands ready’ to release emergency oil reserves
item.description: Finance ministers pledge ‘necessary measures’ to tackle surging crude prices but stop short of immediate deal

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS NO IRAN GROUND MISSION—AND HE’S “NOT HAPPY” WITH NEW LEADER
Donald Trump told The Post that the U.S. is “nowhere near” deploying troops into Iran to secure the nuclear site at Isfahan, dismissing active plans for a ground mission despite media reports of internal discussions.
Trump also said he is “not happy” with the recent appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran’s new supreme leader after the death of his father, calling him unacceptable and declining to detail how he plans to respond.
He reiterated that no decision has been made on U.S. troop deployment in Iran and kept his strategy close to the vest amid escalating geopolitical tensions.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS NO IRAN GROUND MISSION—AND HE’S “NOT HAPPY” WITH NEW LEADER

Donald Trump told The Post that the U.S. is “nowhere near” deploying troops into Iran to secure the nuclear site at Isfahan, dismissing active plans for a ground mission despite media reports of internal discussions.

Trump also said he is “not happy” with the recent appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran’s new supreme leader after the death of his father, calling him unacceptable and declining to detail how he plans to respond.

He reiterated that no decision has been made on U.S. troop deployment in Iran and kept his strategy close to the vest amid escalating geopolitical tensions.
